activities.description: Depart Copenhagen (CPH) → Brussels (BRU) — Flight TBD
activities.details: Flight to be confirmed. ~1h35 nonstop. Suggested departure window ~12:30–13:30 to arrive BRU by ~14:30. Check flysas.com or other carriers once the 2027 schedule is published.

activities.description: Arrival at Brussels Airport (BRU)
activities.details: Brussels Airport (Zaventem) is a single terminal. Baggage claim is typically 20–30 min after landing for Schengen flights.

activities.description: Transfer Brussels Airport → Ghent (~50 min by train or taxi)
activities.details: By train: direct IC Brussels-Airport-Zaventem → Ghent-Sint-Pieters, ~55 min, ~DKK 80 pp each way. By taxi/private transfer: ~DKK 450–670, ~50 min.

activities.description: Check-in at NH Collection Gent TBD
activities.details: Suggested hotel: NH Collection Gent (4-star, central, walking distance from Korenmarkt and Graslei). Booking to be confirmed.

activities.description: Dinner & drinks in Ghent
activities.details: Plenty of choice around Korenmarkt, Graslei and Patershol. The medieval centre is illuminated after dark — worth a walk along the quays.

activities.description: Sightseeing in Ghent: Gravensteen, Graslei, Korenlei
activities.details: Gravensteen (Castle of the Counts) dates to 1180 and offers panoramic views. Graslei and Korenlei are the iconic medieval guild-house quays along the Leie river — the heart of Ghent.

activities.description: Lunch at Artevelde
activities.details: Artevelde — city brewery + restaurant at Botermarkt 5, run by family brewery Huyghe. Weekday €25 lunch menu (soup, choice of three mains incl. veggie, coffee). House beers brewed on-site, including the Artevelde Leute (silver medal at the Brussels Beer Challenge).

activities.description: Beers in Ghent — pick a bar (or take a brewery tour)
activities.details:
Three options for an afternoon round:

Het Waterhuis aan de Bierkant — canal-side beer bar at Groentenmarkt 9 with 165 beers, incl. three house brews (Gandavum, Klokke Roeland, Mammelokker).

Dulle Griet — old-school Flemish pub at Vrijdagmarkt 50 with 500+ beers; trade a shoe as collateral for the tall Kwak glass.

Brewery tour — Visit Gent's brewery guide covers tours at Gruut, Dok Brewing Company (Hal 16), Stropken and more.

Waterhuis ↗ Dulle Griet ↗ Brewery tour ↗

activities.description: Dinner & drinks in Ghent
activities.details: Relaxed evening ahead of the early race-day start. Patershol is full of small restaurants; the quays around Graslei are perfect for an after-dinner drink.

activities.description: Early night — 05:00 wake-up tomorrow!
activities.details: Race day starts brutally early. Aim to be in bed by 21:00 to get a decent night's sleep before the 05:00 alarm.

activities.description: Early wake-up and breakfast
activities.details: Check whether the hotel offers an early breakfast service — arrange this in advance. Alternatively, pastries and coffee are served on the Go4Cycling bus.

activities.description: Meeting point in Ghent – Go4Cycling VIP bus
activities.details: Confirm the exact pick-up point with Go4Cycling before the trip — Go4Cycling typically picks up in central Ghent for Paris-Roubaix bookings. Have your booking confirmation ready.

activities.description: Transfer Ghent → Compiègne (~2h30) with coffee, tea & pastries
activities.details: ~290 km by coach. Compiègne is the traditional start city of Paris-Roubaix since 1977. Pastries and hot drinks served on board.

activities.description: Start area in Compiègne – experience team buses and preparations
activities.details: WorldTour team buses line the start route through the city centre. Riders do sign-on at the podium. A unique chance to get close to the pros before the flag drops.

activities.description: Return to bus
activities.details: Meet back at the agreed bus location promptly — the bus departs before the race start to position at the first viewing point.

activities.description: Bus departs – race starts at 10:15, follow from key positions along the route
activities.details: The peloton is neutralised through Compiègne city before the race goes live. The bus heads to the first strategic roadside position. Go4Cycling plans multiple stops along the course including iconic cobbled sectors — exact locations confirmed closer to race day.

activities.description: Lunch break with packed meal & CAVA on board
activities.details: Lunch is served while the bus repositions between sectors. CAVA and soft drinks included.

activities.description: Live experience at multiple strategic points
activities.details: Key sectors to watch for: Trouée d'Arenberg (km 162, ★★★★★), Mons-en-Pévèle (km 200, ★★★★), Carrefour de l'Arbre (km 238, ★★★★★). Cobblestones are rough — wear comfortable shoes.

activities.description: Arrival at Roubaix Velodrome – finish line!
activities.details: The Vélodrome André Pétrieux dates to 1936 and holds ~8,000 spectators for the finish. VIP access included. The winner's shower in the famous communal showers is a tradition.

activities.description: Return to Ghent
activities.details: ~1h30 drive from Roubaix Velodrome back to Ghent.

activities.description: Dinner & drinks — relive the day's madness
activities.details: Celebrate at a restaurant in Ghent. Consider booking in advance — the area sees race-day crowds on Paris-Roubaix Sunday.

activities.description: Breakfast and hotel check-out
activities.details: Standard checkout is 12:00 noon, but confirm with the hotel. Luggage storage is usually available if you want to explore Ghent in the morning before heading to the airport.

activities.description: Transfer Ghent → Brussels Airport (~50 min by train or taxi)
activities.details: Direct IC train Ghent-Sint-Pieters → Brussels-Airport-Zaventem, ~55 min, ~DKK 80 pp. By taxi/private transfer: ~50 min depending on traffic. Book ahead either way.

activities.description: Arrive Brussels Airport (BRU) · check in for departure
activities.details: Check-in desks close 45 min before departure. Security is typically 20–30 min. SAS desks are in Terminal 1 (Schengen departures).

activities.description: Brussels (BRU) → Copenhagen (CPH) — Flight TBD
activities.details: Flight to be confirmed. ~1h30 nonstop. Suggested departure window ~15:00–16:00 to arrive CPH by ~17:00. Verify at flysas.com or other carriers once the 2027 schedule is published.
